Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Table 1 lists PCK1 with AGI At4g37870.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly refers to Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species.reason: Mutant and transgenic analyses were performed in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experiments are in Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state mutants are in Arabidopsis ecotype Col-0.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Figure 3e measures soluble sugar content; text says sugar levels are reduced in pck1.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Sugar content was measured in 2-day-old seedlings.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states gluconeogenesis fuels post-germinative growth/seedling establishment and sugar rescue restores growth.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PCK1 is directly investigated as part of gluconeogenesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results and Fig. 4 support PCK as the route for C4 dicarboxylic acids, primarily from lipid breakdown, into gluconeogenesis.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: PCK is presented as the entry gateway converting organic acids to PEP.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports PCK as a pivotal/gateway enzyme, but does not explicitly test or name it rate-limiting or a control step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper states PCK catalyses conversion of oxaloacetic acid to PEP.

Primary_Product.reason: PCK produces phosphoenolpyruvate according to the Introduction.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: This reaction description matches the explicit statement in the paper.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PCK is shown to supply gluconeogenic precursors leading to sugar accumulation.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PCK enzyme identity is explicit, though no domain analysis is provided.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: PCK1 is discussed functionally, but this paper does not explicitly state cytosolic localization for PCK1.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Fig. 1a,b and text support induction after germination, peak around 2 days, initial radicle/micropylar aleurone expression, spread to cotyledons/hypocotyl, and persistence in attached aleurone.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Immunoblotting shows strong induction after germination with highest abundance around 2 days in seedlings.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Results describe PCK1::uidA translational fusions and vein-specific enhancer trap/UAS hairpin RNAi constructs.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed factors are in Results/Methods, except ABA repression is only discussed from prior work; overall field remains supported because most items are directly in this paper and ABA is explicitly discussed as prior work.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper uses T-DNA mutants, double mutant, promoter-GUS, immunoblots, metabolite profiling, radiolabel feeding, and complementation context in the double mutant analysis.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show reduced sugar accumulation, reduced establishment under suboptimal light, and major role in lipid-derived carbon use.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered claim is supported by Fig. 1, Fig. 2, Fig. 3e, Fig. 4a,b, and sugar-starvation transcript data for the double mutant.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper links PCK1 to seedling vigor/fitness, but does not explicitly present breeding or engineering applications.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results note delayed rosette growth and flowering in pck1 and severe early growth defect in the double mutant.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper used Affymetrix ATH1 arrays with three microarray experiments per genotype and no accession is reported in the provided text.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Fig. 4 supports stronger importance for lipid-derived substrates, and Supplementary Fig. 2b shows chloroplastic PPDK does not complement the double-mutant defect.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Table 1 lists PPDK with AGI At4g15530.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper explicitly names pyruvate, orthophosphate dikinase (PPDK).

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly refers to Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species.reason: Mutant and transgenic analyses were performed in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experiments are in Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Methods state mutants are in ecotype Col-0.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Figure 3e measures soluble sugar content and shows reduction in ppdk seedlings.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Sugar content was measured in 2-day-old seedlings.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper ties gluconeogenic sugar production to post-germinative growth and seedling establishment.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The central claim is that PPDK functions in gluconeogenesis in Arabidopsis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results and Fig. 4 support PPDK as gateway from pyruvate, mainly from amino-acid/protein breakdown, into gluconeogenesis.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: PPDK is presented as an upstream gateway for organic acid entry to PEP.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper supports PPDK as an important alternative gateway, but does not explicitly establish it as rate-limiting or a control step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: PPDK is described as allowing sugars to be made from pyruvate and as direct phosphorylation of pyruvate.

Primary_Product.reason: PPDK is presented as synthesizing PEP in vivo for gluconeogenesis.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The description matches the paper's functional description, though ATP-to-AMP detail is discussed in Fig. 4e rather than as a full formal reaction.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PPDK supplies precursors for gluconeogenic sugar accumulation.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper states one Arabidopsis PPDK gene encodes both cytosolic and chloroplastic proteins due to alternate promoter elements.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Results explicitly distinguish cytosolic and chloroplastic PPDK isoforms.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Fig. 1a,b and text support induction after germination, early expression in radicle/micropylar aleurone, spread to cotyledons/hypocotyl, peak around 2 days, and persistence later.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper shows strong post-germinative induction in seedlings and coordinated accumulation with PCK1.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Results mention chloroplastic and cytosolic PPDK::uidA translational fusions and vein-specific enhancer trap/UAS hairpin RNAi constructs.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: PDRP is discussed in Discussion as a regulator identified in Arabidopsis, but no interaction or testing is shown in Results.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed growth conditions are directly tested except ABA, which is only a preliminary discussion item; the field is broadly consistent with the paper's treatments.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The paper uses T-DNA mutant analysis, double mutant analysis, complementation with cytosolic versus chloroplastic PPDK, promoter-GUS, immunoblotting, metabolite profiling, and radiolabel feeding.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show lower sugar, elevated alanine, increased CO2 release from alanine, and reduced establishment under suboptimal conditions.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered claim is supported by Fig. 1, Fig. 2, Fig. 3e,f, Fig. 4c,d, and Supplementary Fig. 2b.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper supports relevance to fitness/seedling vigor, but does not explicitly propose engineering deployment.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The higher ATP cost of the PPDK route is discussed, and loss phenotypes under poor conditions and in the double mutant are shown.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper used Affymetrix ATH1 arrays with three microarray experiments per genotype and no accession is given in the text.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The study explicitly identifies PPDK as a eukaryotic gluconeogenic enzyme in Arabidopsis and shows chloroplastic PPDK does not rescue the relevant seedling defect.
